compiled_state.title: [test-batched.sh]: bash runner ignores SUITE_TEST_INDEX RED markers -> validate.sh fails on tests CI tolerates
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

`plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh` (with `--runner=bash`) treats every non-zero test exit as `fail`, regardless of `SUITE_TEST_INDEX` RED markers. The CI's `tests/lib/suite-engine.sh` honors RED markers and reports tolerated failures; the local validate.sh path does not, causing ~25 false-positive failures.

## Reproduction
- Run `SUITE_TEST_INDEX=\$(pwd)/.test-index bash tests/run-all.sh` → PASSED: 4735, FAILED: 1 (CI behavior)
- Run `bash plugins/dso/scripts/validate.sh --ci` → tests step PENDING/FAIL (~25 raw failures)

## Impact
- `scripts/release.sh` precondition #8 (`validate.sh --ci` passes) blocked
- Local dev cannot release while CI is green

## Affected files
- `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/bash-runner.sh` (per-test outcome decision)
- `plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh` (state lifecycle)

## Fix
Add SUITE_TEST_INDEX/RED-marker awareness to bash-runner.sh, mirroring the logic in `tests/lib/suite-engine.sh` lines 95-145, 415-510. When a test exits non-zero AND its function-level RED marker is set, classify as `pass` (tolerated) instead of `fail`.
